name: Config Show Command

tasks.title: Write config show command spec
tasks.body:
Write a spec for the 'wolfcastle config show' command. The spec should define:

- Output format: the resolved config printed as indented JSON to stdout (matching the structure in types.go). In --json mode, wrap in the standard {ok, action, data} envelope.
- Flag: --tier <base|custom|local> to display a single tier's raw content instead of the merged result. Omitting --tier shows the fully merged config (the default and most common use case).
- Flag: --raw to suppress the defaults layer and show only what the tier files actually contain (useful for debugging what a specific tier overrides).
- Behavior: the command must work after 'wolfcastle init' (requires a valid .wolfcastle directory). It calls config.Load() for the merged view and reads individual tier files for --tier mode.
- No model invocation required. Pure config read.
- Error handling: if a tier file is missing (e.g., no local/config.json), print {} for that tier rather than erroring.

Place the spec at docs/specs/ with the standard timestamped filename.

tasks.title: Implement config show command
tasks.body:
Implement the 'wolfcastle config show' command per the spec written in task-0001.

Implementation plan:
- Create cmd/config/register.go following the Pattern B registration convention (see cmd/project/register.go, cmd/audit/ for examples). Register a 'config' parent command with a 'show' subcommand.
- Create cmd/config/show.go with the show subcommand implementation.
- Wire config.Register(app, rootCmd) in cmd/root.go setupCommands(). Assign configCmd.GroupID = "diagnostics" since this is a debugging/introspection tool.
- For the merged view: call app.Config.Load() (already loaded in PersistentPreRunE) and marshal the result as indented JSON.
- For --tier mode: read the raw tier file from the appropriate system/<tier>/config.json path. Return {} if the file doesn't exist.
- For --raw mode with --tier: show only the raw tier file content without merging with defaults.
- Support --json flag via the standard output.Print(output.OK(...)) pattern.
- Write comprehensive tests in cmd/config/show_test.go and cmd/config/register_test.go.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config Show Command is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify the config show command is registered in the diagnostics group alongside doctor and unblock

scope.description: New CLI command: wolfcastle config show
criteria: config show prints valid merged JSON
--json flag wraps output in standard envelope
missing tier files produce {} not errors

breadcrumbs.text: Wrote config show command spec covering merged/tier/raw modes, --json envelope, error handling, and Cobra registration plan
Implemented config show command: cmd/config/{register,show,show_test}.go, wired into root.go. Supports merged/tier/raw modes with --json envelope. 24 tests, 94.7% coverage.

Audit reviewed 4 files (cmd/config/register.go, show.go, show_test.go, cmd/root.go diff) totaling ~475 lines. PASS. Build clean, all 24 tests pass, 94.7% coverage, go vet and gofmt clean, full suite (28 packages) green. No findings requiring remediation. Flag error discards in show.go:36-37 are safe (Cobra guarantees flag existence). Path construction uses hardcoded tier names only, no injection risk. The switch statement correctly prioritizes --tier over --raw. Code is well-factored into small testable functions with clear separation of concerns. Spec is thorough and implementation matches it.

audit.result_summary: Implemented the wolfcastle config show command as a new cmd/config package with three output modes (merged, tier, raw), --json envelope support, and comprehensive error handling. The command is registered under a new 'config' command group in the diagnostics section, with show as its first subcommand. The spec documents all behavior, flags, error cases, and exit codes. 24 tests provide 94.7% coverage across all modes and error paths. Audit found no issues requiring remediation.

audit.objective: Verify all work in the config show command node is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Reviewed cmd/config/register.go, show.go, show_test.go, the root.go diff, and the linked spec. Ran build, vet, fmt, package tests (24 pass, 94.7% coverage), and full suite (28 packages green). Evaluated correctness, error handling, concurrency safety, code quality, and spec conformance.
went_well: Implementation is clean and well-factored into small testable functions
Spec is thorough with concrete examples and matches the code precisely
Error handling covers all edge cases: missing files, malformed JSON, invalid tier names
action_items: Future config subcommands (set, diff) can be added as siblings in cmd/config/register.go

task-0001.objective: Write a spec for the wolfcastle config show command
task-0001.what_happened: Studied the config system (types.go, config.Load, tier file layout, DeepMerge), the JSON envelope format in output/envelope.go, and existing spec conventions. Wrote the spec covering three output modes (merged, tier, raw), the --json envelope, error handling for missing/malformed tier files, exit codes, and Cobra registration as a new config command group.
went_well: The spec covers all six points from the task description with concrete examples
Cross-referenced actual codebase types rather than inventing structure
improvements: Could have included a section on shell completion for --tier values
action_items: Implementation task should create the config command group (new cmd/config/ directory with register.go and show.go)

task-0002.objective: Implement the wolfcastle config show command per the spec from task-0001
task-0002.what_happened: Created cmd/config/register.go (Pattern B registration, diagnostics group), cmd/config/show.go (show subcommand with --tier, --raw, --json flags), and cmd/config/show_test.go (24 tests, 94.7% coverage). Wired config.Register into cmd/root.go setupCommands(). Used tierfs.TierNames as canonical tier list for validation. Constructed tier file paths from app.Config.Root() + system/<tier>/config.json rather than exposing the private tierfs.Resolver. Reused config.DeepMerge for --raw mode.
went_well: Clean separation: readTierFile, mergeRawTiers, and marshalPretty are independently testable
All three output modes (merged, tier, raw) work correctly with both human and JSON output
94.7% test coverage with 24 test cases covering all error paths
improvements: The import alias wcconfig in root.go is necessary because cmd/config collides with internal/config; could be avoided if the package were named differently, but renaming is out of scope
action_items: Future config subcommands (config set, config diff) can be added to cmd/config/register.go as siblings of show
